---
title: "The ethics of science communication in a polarised world"
page-layout: article
toc: false
---

<div class="sc-page-hero">
  <p class="sc-eyebrow">Episode 40 · Apr 7, 2025</p>
  <h1>The ethics of science communication in a polarised world</h1>
  <div style="display:flex;gap:14px;margin-top:16px;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:13px;color:var(--ink-faint);">
    <span>⏱ 44 min</span><span>🎙 Amir Khan, Sara Lindqvist</span>
  </div>
  <div style="display:flex;gap:10px;margin-top:20px;">
    <a href="#" class="btn btn-dark">🎙 Listen on Spotify</a>
    <a href="#" class="btn btn-outline">Apple Podcasts</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div style="max-width:700px;margin:0 auto;padding:0 40px 60px;">

When the public is divided along political lines, how do communicators share inconvenient truths without triggering defensive reactions? A nuanced look at the psychology of persuasion and what the evidence says about changing minds.

</div>

<section class="sc-about-strip">
  <h2>More episodes</h2>
  <a href="index.qmd" class="btn btn-outline">All episodes →</a>
</section>
